history.content:
<thought>
From my position, the cell directly in front is a wall (#) (the column with # extends upward), so I cannot move forward. I should turn right to try to find an open path around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see lava (L) to my front-left area and walls (#) to the sides; the immediate cell in front of me appears to be a wall (#), so moving forward is unsafe/impossible. I will turn left to look for a floor (.) route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back to the earlier orientation: the cell in front is a wall (#), and lava is nearby to the left side, so I should not move forward. I will turn right again to escape the wall and look for a safe floor path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The observation matches a state where the cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so MOVE_FORWARD is not possible. To find a navigable corridor (.) or the goal (G), I will turn right to change direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can only see the bottom portion of the corridor: there is a wall (#) immediately in front of me, and everything around is unknown (?) except the wall. Since forward is blocked, I should turn to find an opening or the goal in another direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view shows a wall (#) directly in front of me again, so moving forward is impossible. The safe way to progress is to change orientation; I will turn right to look for a non-wall adjacent cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The local view is the same as before: a wall (#) is in front of me, so I can’t move forward. I’ll rotate left to explore the other side for a possible path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
A wall (#) is directly in front of me, so I still cannot move forward. To change which cell is in front, I will rotate right again.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back to a view where there is a wall directly in front of me, so I still can’t move forward. I should rotate the other way to try a different direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view appears unchanged: a wall (#) is still directly in front of me, so moving forward is impossible. I should rotate left to try a different heading.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a clear floor cell directly in front of me ('.') with no lava, while walls are to the right/left. So I should move forward to advance along the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
